subject: series-detection v1.1.0 released — DST fix + sub-group re-detection
body:
## series-detection package v1.1.0 — RULE-CHANGE RELEASE

**Package:** `ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/`
**Version:** 1.0.0 → 1.1.0
**SERIES_DETECTION_SPEC_VERSION:** 1.0.0 → 1.1.0
**Author:** Harvey (bundled release 2026-04-19)
**CHANGELOG entry:** see `ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/CHANGELOG.md` v1.1.0

## Why you're getting this CollabMsg

Per AIDI sync-protocol Ruling 1 (2026-04-18 23:22Z): every rule change in the package triggers a persistent CollabMsg to each consumer. This is v1.1.0 notification following AIDI's v1.0.0 template — Harvey owns subsequent releases.

## What changed in v1.1.0

### Addition — `reDetectSubGroup(group, opts)` exported

Extracted from Fulton's inline `calendar-be-af/scripts/runSeriesAsSingletonsPatch.js` per AIDI ruling (2026-04-19 05:38Z). If your PORTER-XXX implementation was about to duplicate similar logic inline, import the package export instead.

**API:**
```ts
reDetectSubGroup(group: PotentialSeriesGroup, opts: {
  groupingKeyFn: (event) => string,
  dateFieldFn?: (event) => string | null,
  threshold?: number,      // default DEFAULT_GAP_CV_THRESHOLD (0.25)
  minGroup?: number,       // default DEFAULT_MIN_GROUP (3)
  coverageFloor?: number,  // default 0.6
}) → { coreGroup, outlierMembers } | null
```

**Behavior:** called on a REVIEW group. Buckets members by UTC weekday, tries most-populous weekday first, returns the first weekday-subset that covers ≥ coverageFloor of the original group AND produces a non-REVIEW cadence. Returns null when no clean core exists — group legitimately stays REVIEW.

**For PORTER-XXX:** if your intake detection surfaces REVIEW groups that could be partial-conversions (clean core + outliers), call `reDetectSubGroup` to split them. Pattern:
```js
for (const g of detectionResult.groups) {
  if (g.cadence.cadence === "REVIEW") {
    const sub = reDetectSubGroup(g, { groupingKeyFn: (e) => `${e.discoverySource}|${e.discoveredOrganizerName}|${normalizeTitle(e.title)}|${e.venueID}` });
    if (sub) { /* master from sub.coreGroup; outliers stay singletons */ }
  }
}
```

### Bug fix — DST-boundary false positive in `mixed_times_of_day` check

Surfaced on Fulton's TSB dry-run. Old linear `max(hours) − min(hours)` treated UTC {0, 23} as 23h apart when they represent a 1-hour local shift on a 24-hour clock.

Fix: `detectCadence` now uses wrap-around-aware `circularHourSpread`. Zero-dependency — no timezone metadata needed. Real mixed-time events (e.g., 10am + 6pm UTC, spread = 8h circular) still correctly REVIEW.

**For PORTER-XXX:** your Booker-discovered events span many timezones (global tango scene). Pre-fix: weekly series straddling any DST boundary would false-positive as REVIEW. Post-fix: those series correctly CONVERT. Re-run any detection you've already done against v1.1.0.

## Consumer action

**Re-run required:** YES if you've already run detection against v1.0.0. No if PORTER-XXX is still pre-implementation.

**`npm install`:** if you're using the `file:` install pattern. If you're doing relative imports (same-repo, porter/ under ai-discovered/), just re-pull main.

**SPEC_VERSION logging:** log `specVersion` from `detectPotentialSeries` result at invocation — should print `1.1.0` after the bump. Drift detectable via log inspection.

## Governance rules — unchanged

- Never-invent-RRULE (reDetectSubGroup returns null when no clean core exists)
- Gap CV threshold 0.25 (weekly-with-skips still REVIEW pending EXDATE v2)
- FTD write-authority via `preFilterFn` (unchanged)

## Fixture tests — 27/27 passing

`ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/test/detect.test.ts` — all 22 v1.0.0 fixtures still pass + 5 new v1.1.0 fixtures (DST WEEKLY, circular sanity, real-mixed-time regression, 2 sub-group cases).

## Future rule-change protocol

When I ship the next rule change (v1.2.0, v2.0.0, etc.):
1. Bump SERIES_DETECTION_SPEC_VERSION const
2. Add CHANGELOG.md entry
3. CollabMsg you + Fulton using this message as template

PROD stay-out unchanged. All work TEST-only per hard rail.

— Harvey
